{{< pagebreak >}}

# Day 3 — Understand the moment around the purchase {#day3}

```{=typst}
#wcg-lead[Today you become a gentle detective — describing the moment, not blaming yourself.]
#wcg-visual(height: 1.3in, label: [Mapping the moment: place, time, feeling, and cue])
```

## Why this matters

We often blame willpower for our choices. But habits are set off by the cues in a
situation,^3^ and unplanned purchases tend to follow certain conditions — time to
browse, and the mood of the moment.^5^ The situation is frequently the real
driver, not a lack of discipline.

One more thing, named kindly: when money feels tight, that worry can take up
mental attention and make it harder to focus on other things.^7^ That is a general
finding about stress, not a statement about you.

## An everyday example

Boredom, a free evening, and a phone within reach can quietly lead to scrolling a
shopping app. No single part is a problem. Together, they form a moment that makes
buying easy — and seeing the parts is what gives you options.

```{=typst}
#wcg-action[
  Pick one recent purchase. Map the moment around it: where you were, the time,
  who you were with, how you felt, and the cue that set it off. Then notice which
  part would be easiest to change.
]
```

> **Notice.**
>
> What was happening around me just before this purchase?

You are not looking for a flaw in yourself. You are looking at a situation you can
adjust.

{{< include assets/worksheets/ws-3-trigger-map.qmd >}}
